seoTitle: Clarke Creek vs Clear Island Waters — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clarke Creek and Clear Island Waters. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Clarke Creek vs Clear Island Waters: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clarke Creek and Clear Island Waters.
heading: Clarke Creek vs Clear Island Waters — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clarke Creek, Queensland and Clear Island Waters, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clear Island Waters has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Clarke Creek (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clarke Creek or Clear Island Waters safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clarke Creek has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Clear Island Waters scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clarke Creek scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Clear Island Waters scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clarke Creek or Clear Island Waters?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clear Island Waters has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
